text: This video shows sagittal views of dynamic MRI scans performed on three patients to assess the status of pelvic floor reconstructions with biological mesh. After an extra levator abdominoperineal excision of rectal cancer, a dynamic MRI video for a patient with a perineal hernia is included at the end of the video to demonstrate abdominal pelvic floor reconstructions and mechanisms.

Dynamic MRI footage shows the pelvic floor reconstruction with Stratus biological mesh in a male patient post-extra levator abdominoperineal excision of rectal cancer. A red arrow highlights the reconstructed pelvic floor, which remains stable without herniation or abnormal descent at rest and during the Valsalva maneuver.

Sagittal views of dynamic MRI for a male patient show the reconstructed pelvic floor with a red pointer. At rest and during a Valsalva maneuver, there is no perineal herniation or abnormal descent of the pelvic floor.

The MRI footage displays a perineal hernia through a pelvic floor defect following an extra levator abdominoperineal excision of rectal cancer. At rest, the hernia is visible, and during the Valsalva maneuver, further herniation of the small bowel into the perineal defect is observed.
